confirmed.note: validateTransparency = copy of validateAnnotation: identical centre-pixel scan loop + threshold + PASS/FAIL prints, only the counter var name and message strings renamed; main() camera/light/offscreen-render scaffold also copy-edited between the two files in same commit

false_positives.what_happened: OBOL_NO_OPENGL stub file: ~600 no-op SoGLContext_glXxx() wrappers, each body is just (void)glue;(void)arg1;... So any token/line detector sees hundreds of near-identical within-file chunks (rename of the parameter-discard list). This is mechanical dispatch-stub boilerplate, not a copied-and-edited algorithm. File header self-declares it as a stub replacement for gl.cpp.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Add a 'trivial-body' filter to #056: skip chunks whose statements are exclusively (void)<id>; casts / empty returns / single return-of-member. Also treat a file whose function bodies are >80% (void)-discard or single-statement as a dispatch/stub surface and suppress within-file chunk reports for it (analogous to the generated-header marker, but body-shape based since stubs lack a generator banner).

false_positives.what_happened: A family of *_warning() functions differing only by the env-var string literal (OBOL_GLGLUE_NO_RADEON_WARNING, ..._G400_..., ..._ELSA_..., etc). Detector flags 6 near-identical 8-line bodies as within-file copy-edit, but this is an env-var lookup table unrolled into functions (one row per driver). Also: gl.cpp carries the upstream Kongsberg/Coin3D copyright header and is the renamed-to-OBOL vendor dispatch glue, not freshly authored code.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Treat string-literal-only divergence as a config/data table: if two candidate chunks are identical except for one or more string/enum literal tokens (no control-flow or identifier-binding differences), down-weight them as data-table rows rather than copied logic. Combine with a copyright-header heuristic: files whose top comment matches a known vendor license boilerplate (Kongsberg/Coin3D) get vendor-suppressed even when renamed (COIN_->OBOL_).

false_positives.what_happened: All render_*.cpp tests open with the same harness preamble: #include headless_utils.h + SoOrthographicCamera, static const int W/H=256, initCoinHeadless(), identical camera position/near/far/height setup. Cross-file token overlap on this shared test-harness idiom can trip a low-threshold detector, but the actual per-test logic (test1_..testN_, validators) differs per file.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Anchor #056 cross-file matching to function-body spans, not file-prefix windows: exclude includes + top-of-main fixture setup (calls into a shared headless/setup helper) from the hashed token stream. Maintain a small per-corpus 'fixture preamble' allowlist (initCoinHeadless + standard camera setup) so the recurring test boilerplate is normalized away before similarity scoring.

cycle.desc: no author include-cycle reproducible with archcheck --graph
cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph /home/localadm/oss/_aidev_dense/starseeker_obol reports sccs_cyclic:0, largest_scc:1 (nodes:236, edges:20). The CORPUS_CHECK_DETAIL claim of 4 cycles/SCC=6 is not reproducible on the current tree: nearly all includes are <Inventor/...> resolved against include/ on the build include-path, which the path-less preview scan cannot resolve (external:2449, unresolved:320), so no real cyclic edge is observable. There is also no top-level upstream/ directory (the report's upstream/* sources are absent from the working tree), so the cited matches against upstream/* cannot be re-checked either. No author-code cycle confirmed.
